features.title: Milestone A — Core flows (builder, grader, runner, persistence)
features.notes: Selection, grading, and Dexie-backed test/session wiring implemented across builder, grader, create-test, and runner UIs.

features.title: Analytics helpers baseline + tests
features.notes: Overall/topic accuracy helpers hardened with Vitest coverage to support upcoming analytics UI.

features.title: CRUD editor polish
features.notes: Authoring form now narrows MCQ/PBQ unions, duplicates per discriminant, and seeds use canonical types.

features.title: Authoring type safety
features.notes: Centralised question discriminated unions with guards and updated UI consumers to avoid unsafe property access.

features.title: Analytics windows alignment
features.notes: Overall accuracy windows now expose seven-day key consistently across analytics UI and helpers.

features.title: Acronym hangman practice
features.notes: Standalone acronym hangman mode started from Home; acronyms stored in IndexedDB with PDF/text import, plus optional auto-load (and retry) from a local PDF in public/acronyms/security-plus.pdf (PDF parsing handles multi-page + multi-column tables). Masked definitions preserve word spacing for multi-word answers. Includes an Acronyms manager page for viewing and CRUD edits.

features.title: Import/Export JSON (merge/replace)
features.notes: Settings page supports offline JSON export plus merge/replace imports with schema validation and warnings.

features.title: Test runner UX enhancements
features.notes: Retry missed-only flow, optional timer, keyboard help modal, explanation display after submit, plus centered runner layout, question navigator, review screen, progress summaries, and unsaved-answer guards are implemented.

features.title: Dexie migration v2 (difficulty + config)
features.notes: Added difficulty field normalization and a config store for mastery threshold + timer settings; DB now at v3.

features.title: Practice exam PDF import (questions)
features.notes: Question Bank can import ExamsDigest-style Security+ practice exam PDFs into IndexedDB, creating needed topics and surfacing parse warnings (handles merged “Explanation … Correct Answer …” lines, question headers missing punctuation, and common explanation-number typos in the source PDF). Dedupes identical questions across sections (merges topic tags, keeps best explanation) and labels the 401-600 ranges as Exam Simulator #5/#6. Includes a one-click question bank dedupe action to remove any remaining duplicates and rewrite related tests/attempts.

features.title: Security+ lesson coverage expansion
features.notes: Guided lessons now use Professor Messer course notes as the main content base via a generated 119-lesson section path (1.1 through 5.6), plus a supplemental 4.9 investigation-data lesson from the other local Security+ materials. The repo also includes reusable PDF extraction and lesson-generation scripts plus local text extractions in docs/extracted for future content work.

features.title: Security+ gap remediation supplements
features.notes: Added anchored supplemental lessons and merged same-number addenda back into their base lessons, with a standalone 4.9 investigation lesson retained separately. Coverage now explicitly includes LDAP injection, X.500 distinguished names, Kerberos KDC/TGT/TGS plus clock-skew dependency, API gateways, STARTTLS, SFTP vs FTPS, SMTP 25/587/465 roles, SNMPv3, TACACS+, PLC/DCS/HMI/data historian, Purdue Model, split tunneling, geofencing/mobile threat defense, DPP, juice jacking, gamified awareness delivery, CMDB usage, and SED/TCG Opal details.

features.title: Playwright lesson verification
features.notes: Added Playwright config, scripts, and a Chromium E2E pass that walks every lesson and every slide to catch render breakage, empty content, browser errors, and obvious placeholder text in the lesson viewer.

features.title: Targeted lesson diagram corrections
features.notes: Added the missing Race Conditions diagram from Professor Messer PDF page 32 and corrected Lesson 62 slide 4 to use/crop Official Student Guide page 117 instead of page 118.

features.title: Lesson diagram explanations and scaling
features.notes: Added the missing Lesson 62 access-rules explanation slide and changed diagram rendering to re-measure width and preserve aspect ratio instead of stretching cropped canvases.

features.title: Encoding normalization
features.notes: Normalized the remaining mojibake sequences in the generated lesson data, diagram caption map, and local metadata so user-facing lesson content renders with the intended punctuation and symbols again.

features.title: Diagram cutoff diagnosis
features.notes: Playwright verification showed the lesson slide viewport is fitting diagram canvases inside the available space; the remaining visible cutoff is in per-diagram crop definitions, and the diagram E2E check also has a loading-state timing race.

features.title: Diagram renderer stability
features.notes: Diagram slides now measure a stable render stage, keep the current canvas mounted during resizes, and show loading/error text as overlays so PDF renders do not loop back into a stuck loading state.

features.title: Lesson 62 slide 2 diagram fit
features.notes: Expanded the Lesson 62 slide 2 crop to the full security-zone topology figure and gave the lesson slide wrapper explicit full height so diagram panels can render at the intended aspect ratio.

features.title: Diagram dev-override parity
features.notes: Lesson slides now read the same local diagram crop overrides as the dev inspector, keyed by lessonId and slide index, so a crop tuned in the dev menu matches the lesson rendering immediately.

features.title: Diagram crop persistence and stable IDs
features.notes: Diagram slides now get stable IDs so local crop overrides survive lesson reordering, old slide-index overrides are migrated automatically, and the debug inspector can export/import override JSON for reuse across browser profiles.

features.title: Security+ diagram and screenshot expansion
features.notes: Added verified lesson visuals from the Official Student Guide for NIST CSF, control functional types, file-hash verification, digital envelope/PFS, OSI layering, certificate chain-of-trust, remote-access VPN, 802.1X/RADIUS/EAP, SDN planes, Wi-Fi heat maps, Security Onion views, Joe Sandbox, DMARC verification, phishing header analysis, pass-the-hash with LSASS/SAM, the incident-response lifecycle, the cyber kill chain, qualitative risk heat maps, and BIA recovery metrics.

todos.title: Question Editor CRUD + validation
todos.notes: Build full authoring surface with duplicate detection and PBQ JSON validation.

todos.title: Analytics UI (charts + summaries)
todos.notes: Visualise accuracy, weak topics, and history, including CSV export.
